figures.caption: Fig. 4. Qualitative ETMS attention comparison. Both branches share similar global response centers but differ in directional emphasis: Cross-Scan is more axis-aligned, whereas TopoA-Scan responds more strongly to oblique and curved relations. This figure is qualitative only; quantitative evidence is provided by the controlled ablations and topology-oriented analysis.

Fig. 1. Motivation and overview of TopoMamba. Left: axis-biased scanning disrupts non-axial structural continuity in 3D CT, endoscopy, and dermoscopy. Right: Cross-Scan and TopoA-Scan are fused by the HSIC Gate to better preserve non-axial continuity and suppress false positives.

Fig. 2. Architecture of TopoMamba-3D. (a) 3D U-shaped segmentation network with patch embedding, hierarchical TopoMamba blocks, patch merging and expanding, and skip connections. (b) TopoMamba block with TopoA-Scan and Cross-Scan coupled via ScanCache, state-space sequence modeling, HSIC Gate fusion, view-aware reweighting, and a 3D feed-forward module.
Fig. 6. Representative ISIC 2017 and CVC-ClinicDB test cases (fixed random seed). Additional random cases are provided in the supplementary material.

Fig. 3. Effective receptive fields (ERFs) [38] before and after training, averaged over 300 slices. ERFs are computed from unit input perturbations and normalized output-gradient energy. Fusion-Scan denotes Cross-Scan fused with TopoA-Scan via the HSIC Gate.
